evals.prompt: Generate API documentation for our NestJS REST API. We have 15 endpoints across users, products, and orders modules. Include request/response examples, auth requirements, error codes, and pagination format.
evals.expected_output: Comprehensive API docs with endpoint reference, examples, auth guide, and error code table

assertions.name: all_endpoints
assertions.description: All 15 endpoints documented with method, path, description

assertions.name: request_examples
assertions.description: Request body examples for POST/PUT endpoints

assertions.name: response_examples
assertions.description: Response body examples for each endpoint

assertions.name: auth_guide
assertions.description: Authentication section explaining JWT Bearer flow

assertions.name: error_codes
assertions.description: Error code table with status codes and messages

evals.prompt: Generate an OpenAPI/Swagger spec from our NestJS controllers. The API has auth endpoints (login, register, refresh) and CRUD endpoints for tasks with filtering and pagination.
evals.expected_output: OpenAPI 3.0 YAML/JSON spec with paths, schemas, security, and examples

assertions.name: openapi_format
assertions.description: Valid OpenAPI 3.0 spec in YAML or JSON

assertions.name: paths_defined
assertions.description: All API paths with operations defined

assertions.name: schemas_defined
assertions.description: Request/response schemas in components

assertions.name: security_scheme
assertions.description: JWT Bearer security scheme defined

assertions.name: pagination_params
assertions.description: Query parameters for pagination documented

evals.prompt: Create a quick-start guide for our public API. A developer should be able to: get an API key, make their first request, understand the response format, and handle errors. Keep it under 2 pages.
evals.expected_output: Concise quick-start guide with curl examples, API key setup, and error handling

assertions.name: api_key_setup
assertions.description: Step-by-step API key creation instructions

assertions.name: first_request
assertions.description: curl example for the simplest API call

assertions.name: response_format
assertions.description: Response shape explained with example

assertions.name: error_handling
assertions.description: Common errors and what to do about them

assertions.name: concise
assertions.description: Under 200 lines, scannable format

evals.prompt: Generate SDK usage examples for our API in JavaScript and Python. Cover: authentication, listing resources with pagination, creating a resource, handling errors, and webhook setup.
evals.expected_output: Code examples in both JS and Python for common API operations

assertions.name: two_languages
assertions.description: Examples in both JavaScript and Python

assertions.name: auth_example
assertions.description: Authentication/API key setup example

assertions.name: pagination_example
assertions.description: Paginated list request with cursor or page handling

assertions.name: create_example
assertions.description: Resource creation with request body

assertions.name: error_handling_example
assertions.description: Try/catch with specific error types

evals.prompt: Our API docs are outdated — endpoints were added 3 months ago but docs weren't updated. Scan our NestJS controllers and generate a diff showing what's documented vs what's actually implemented.
evals.expected_output: Comparison of existing docs vs actual controllers, listing undocumented endpoints

assertions.name: scans_controllers
assertions.description: Reads NestJS controller decorators to find actual endpoints

assertions.name: reads_existing_docs
assertions.description: Reads existing API documentation

assertions.name: identifies_gaps
assertions.description: Lists endpoints in code but not in docs

assertions.name: identifies_stale
assertions.description: Lists endpoints in docs but removed from code

assertions.name: update_recommendations
assertions.description: Specific recommendations for what to add/remove
